scenarios.title: [DASHBOARD-1] - Check that the welcome banner shows the logged-in user's name @positive
scenarios.userAction: Load the Acme Shop dashboard as an authenticated user (A/B variant forced ON via fixture per Q5) and verify the welcome banner is visible and contains the user's display name.
expectedAssertions: welcome banner element is visible after dashboard load

scenarios.title: [DASHBOARD-2] - Check that the notifications widget shows the most recent notification @positive
scenarios.userAction: Load the Acme Shop dashboard as an authenticated user, click the notifications widget, and verify at least one notification item is visible containing expected text.
expectedAssertions: notifications widget element is visible before interaction
after clicking the widget, at least one notification item is visible
first visible notification item text matches /new order/i

locatorTable.notes: Assumes login form has <label for='email'> or aria-label='Email'. Moves to auth fixture. Fall back: getByRole('textbox', { name: /email/i }). See pin 1.
Same label assumption. Moves to auth fixture. Fall back: getByRole('textbox', { name: /password/i }). See pin 2.
Already canonical in source; minor regex upgrade for copy-variation resilience. Moves to auth fixture.
No DOM snapshot. Hallucination-defense rule 4 prohibits inventing a role without evidence. Retained as CSS class with WHY comment. Reviewer to add data-testid='welcome-banner'. See pin 3.
Element is clicked in source — inferred as interactive. Assumes button role with accessible name including 'notifications'. See pin 4.
No DOM snapshot. Cannot determine element role or accessible name. Retained with // TODO: fragile selector comment. Reviewer to add data-testid='notification-item'. See pin 5.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q3 unresolved: email input label association not confirmed from source — assumed <label for='email'> or aria-label='Email'.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Confirm <label for='email'> exists in login form DOM. If absent, use getByRole('textbox', { name: /email/i }) or keep locator('#email').

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q3 unresolved: password input label association not confirmed — assumed <label for='password'> or aria-label='Password'.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Same as pin 1: confirm label association or add aria-label='Password'.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q4 unresolved: .welcome-banner ARIA role unknown — CSS class last-resort retained, see plan pin 3.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Inspect DOM for ARIA role/testid. Ask FE to add data-testid='welcome-banner', then upgrade to getByTestId('welcome-banner').

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q4 unresolved: .notifications-widget role assumed button (element is clicked in source). Accessible name assumed to contain 'notifications'.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: If not a button or accessible name differs: keep locator('.notifications-widget'), add WHY-comment 'Q4 unresolved: widget role not confirmed — CSS class fallback'.

hallucinationDefensePins.whyComment: Q4 unresolved: .notification-item accessible role unknown — CSS class + .first() last-resort fallback, see plan pin 5.
hallucinationDefensePins.reviewerFallback: Ask FE to add data-testid='notification-item'. Then upgrade to getByTestId('notification-item').first().
